diff_quality.notes: Well-scoped Phase B integration. Client holds no CometChat secrets — appId/region/short-lived authToken are fetched at runtime from POST /cometchat/token and consumed via loginWithAuthToken; .env is gitignored (belt-and-suspenders). Native config is appropriate and clearly commented: Android minSdk 26/compileSdk 35, camera/mic/foreground-service perms, and an app-level network_security_config.xml to re-permit dev-backend cleartext after the SDK merges its own config; iOS deployment target 15.1, usage strings, VoIP background modes, and a Podfile post_install clearing EXCLUDED_ARCHS for the arm64 simulator. The main.dart switch to ChangeNotifierProvider.value + eager Session/CometChatService/CallManager construction is CometChat-wiring-related, not an unrelated refactor. Chat uses UI Kit widgets (Conversations/MessageList/MessageComposer); calling is a hand-rolled surface on the raw cometchat_calls_sdk because the prebuilt calls UI Kit is 4.x-bound.

coverage_gaps: Flutter v5 raw calls: the prebuilt cometchat_calls_uikit is 4.x-bound/incompatible with the v5 Calls SDK, forcing a hand-rolled call surface (joinSession + SessionStatusListeners + CometChatOngoingCallService + clearActiveCall teardown). No docs-mcp/skill content covers building the v5 raw Calls-SDK call UI and mandatory callee-side teardown for Flutter.

Backend JSP/servlet auth-token minting (POST /cometchat/token → {appId,region,uid,role,authToken} using the server-only REST API key) is documented in the README/consumed by the client but no docs-mcp query was recorded and no server code is in the mobile diff, so the server side is asserted, not evidenced.

blockers.issue:
iOS screen infinite-loops / re-initializes — @StateObject wraps a shared singleton service and a .task with no id re-fires on every re-render ([ios] Maestro assertion failed [chat.yaml] on ios: 
Waiting for flows to complete...
[Failed] logistics-ios-chat-smoke (1s)

1/1 Flow Failed


)

debug_agent.root_cause: The gate-3 iOS chat smoke did not fail inside the app — it failed at Maestro `launchApp` because the flow targeted a bundle id that is not installed. The rendered flow (smoke/flows/logistics/ios/chat.yaml -> executed _reports/.../ios/ios/chat.yaml line 5) uses `appId: com.northwind.northwind_dispatch`, taken from the single shared `smoke.app_package` in config/use_cases/05-logistics.yaml:47. But the built iOS app's CFBundleIdentifier is `com.northwind.northwindDispatch` (ios/Runner.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj:513 / built Runner.app Info.plist). Reproduced directly: `xcrun simctl launch <udid> com.northwind.northwind_dispatch` => FBSOpenApplicationServiceErrorDomain code=4 (the exact 1s 'Unknown error' + status=ERROR in chat.yaml.junit.xml, app bundle id absent from ios_unified_log.txt), while `...northwindDispatch` launches to a stable PID 40816 with no crash/relaunch loop. Android runs the identical stub flow and PASSES because its applicationId (android/app/build.gradle.kts:20) is snake_case `com.northwind.northwind_dispatch`. The harness even records the correct iOS id at interop.platforms.ios.app_package (05-logistics.yaml:72) but the smoke section applies one shared app_package to both platforms; Flutter's own scaffolding camelCases the iOS bundle id while keeping Android snake_case, so a single value cannot be right for both. The I1 cluster label (@StateObject/.task SwiftUI re-init loop) is a misattribution: this is a Flutter app with no SwiftUI.

verification.reran: xcrun simctl install + launch on booted iPhone 17 Pro (5C6BDEC6...): launch com.northwind.northwind_dispatch => FBSOpenApplicationServiceErrorDomain code=4 (reproduces the 1s failure); launch com.northwind.northwindDispatch => PID 40816, stable after 2s (app healthy). Android chat.yaml.out.log => Passed.

blockers.retraction_reason: MISDIAGNOSIS — the stated mechanism cannot apply to this app. The finding blames SwiftUI `@StateObject` wrapping a singleton and a `.task` without an id re-firing, but logistics mobile is a FLUTTER app (Dart); it contains no SwiftUI, no @StateObject and no .task. The observed iOS symptom was traced to a stale simulator build: `flutter build ios` silently reinstalled the PREVIOUS Runner.app when ios/Flutter/ephemeral was read-only, so the app ran with the wrong API_BASE_URL and could not reach the backend. Retracted as an app/skills defect; the real issue is the build/harness trap already recorded separately.

blockers.issue:
iOS ghost call — the UI Kit's ongoing-call screen is not dismissed when the remote party ends a 1:1 call; the local user is stuck in a conference-of-one with the timer running until they manually hang up ([ios] Maestro assertion failed [calling.yaml] on ios: 
Waiting for flows to complete...
[Failed] logistics-ios-calling-smoke (1s)

1/1 Flow Failed


)

iOS: only the first call per app launch connects — after any completed call, the next accepted call never transitions to the ongoing screen (sticks at Calling / silently drops); kit-internal state breaks and only a full app restart recovers ([ios] Maestro assertion failed [calling.yaml] on ios: 
Waiting for flows to complete...
[Failed] logistics-ios-calling-smoke (1s)

1/1 Flow Failed


)

debug_agent.root_cause: The iOS Maestro smoke flows launch via `appId: {{app_package}}`, and the harness use-case config (config/use_cases/05-logistics.yaml) sets a single `app_package: com.northwind.northwind_dispatch` used verbatim for both platforms. But `flutter create` generates the iOS bundle id by stripping underscores and camelCasing, so the built/installed iOS app's CFBundleIdentifier is `com.northwind.northwindDispatch` (confirmed in build/ios/iphonesimulator/Runner.app/Info.plist and ios/Runner.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj:513; present since Phase A commit cb5a03b, i.e. pre-CometChat). Maestro's `launchApp` on iOS therefore targets an uninstalled bundle id and fails immediately — reproduced directly on the same booted sim (5C6BDEC6...): `simctl launch com.northwind.northwind_dispatch` → FBSOpenApplicationServiceErrorDomain code=4, while `simctl launch com.northwind.northwindDispatch` succeeds (PID 41359). This is why BOTH iOS flows (chat.yaml AND calling.yaml) fail identically with 'Unknown error / Failed (1s)' and the app never appears in ios_unified_log.txt, while Android (applicationId keeps the underscore, matching) passes. The I5 'second call breaks' fingerprint is a misattribution: a pre-launch, pre-call harness bundle-id mismatch, not a calling regression.

verification.reran: xcrun simctl launch 5C6BDEC6-777C-4F7B-A6AC-1633AB65C9F4 com.northwind.northwind_dispatch (Maestro appId) vs com.northwind.northwindDispatch (built bundle id)

blockers.retraction_reason: CONTRADICTED BY LATER EVIDENCE — 'only the first call per app launch connects; only a full app restart recovers' does not reproduce. At CP2 the operator ran the full manual two-device matrix and 1:1 calls MULTIPLE (a second call placed after the first ends) passed on all three pairs (web-android, android-ios, ios-web), 18/18. A live android<->iOS second call was also observed connecting in-session (accepted -> onParticipantJoined -> onSessionJoined) with no app restart. Retracted on own-evidence contradiction.

blockers.issue: ios smoke drove the pre-installed app — the built sim app is --no-codesign (I9: keychain -34018 breaks CometChat login), so it is NOT auto-installed and freshness is UNVERIFIED ([ios] /Users/admin/Desktop/Allapplications/logistics/mobile/build/ios/iphonesimulator/Runner.app)

Visual defect: Expected an incoming-call UI, but the screen shows the app's login screen instead — no call surface is present (screenshot /Users/admin/Desktop/orchestrator/cometchat-skills-reviewer/cometchat-skills-reviewer/_reports/2026-07-08-baseline/logistics/android-ios/smoke/android/shots/calling.yaml.png: Expected an incoming-call UI, but the screen shows the app's login screen instead — no call surface is present | judge quoted: '"Northwind Dispatch", "Sign in", "DEMO ACCOUNTS", "Tap a role to sign in — shared password \\"password\\"."')
blockers.retraction_reason: MISATTRIBUTION — caused by an UNAUTHORED smoke flow, not the app. As established by the harness-error blockers in this same report, smoke/flows/logistics/{android,ios}/calling.yaml contains only `launchApp: {clearState: true}`; clearState wipes the app to the login screen and the flow ends without signing in or navigating, so the screenshot judge necessarily saw a login screen. The flow measured nothing about the skills under review. Retracted as a skills finding; the harness gap remains recorded.

blockers.issue: Visual defect: Expected a chat thread with message bubbles, but the screen shows a route list (Route Board), not a conversation (screenshot /Users/admin/Desktop/orchestrator/cometchat-skills-reviewer/cometchat-skills-reviewer/_reports/2026-07-08-baseline/logistics/android-ios/smoke/android/shots/chat.yaml.png: Expected a chat thread with message bubbles, but the screen shows a route list (Route Board), not a conversation | judge quoted: '"Route Board", "rte-001", "2026-07-20 · driver usr-driver", "Planned", "2 stops"')
blockers.retraction_reason: MISATTRIBUTION — same unauthored-stub cause as above (chat.yaml is `launchApp` only). The same stub yielded login, 'Route Board' and a blank frame across runs, which is dispositive that the FLOW, not the app, controlled the screen. The app wires a Chats tab on every role opening the real CometChatConversations UI Kit. Retracted as a skills finding; the harness gap remains recorded.

blockers.issue: 1:1 direct thread/call opens the counterparty with the wrong display name: route_detail_screen.dart calls _openDirect(context, directUid!, directLabel) and constructs cc.User(uid: uid, name: name) where `name` is the UI label ('Message driver (1:1)' / 'Message dispatcher (1:1)'), not the person's real name. The MessagesScreen header and the outgoing call title therefore literally read 'Message driver (1:1)' instead of the driver/dispatcher name.

Route/exception threads are entered by pushing client-fabricated stub cc.Group(guid:'route-<id>'/'exception-<id>', type:'private') and cc.User(uid:...) objects straight into CometChatMessageList / call APIs without fetching or joining the target first. Correct behavior depends entirely on the backend having provisioned membership under the mirrored id scheme; unlike ChatsInboxScreen (which has an EmptyState fallback), these screens have no user-facing failure path if the group is unprovisioned or the join fails.

HARNESS BUG (not app/SDK/skills): the logistics Gate 3 mobile smoke flows are unimplemented stubs. smoke/flows/logistics/{android,ios}/{chat,calling}.yaml each contain only `- launchApp: {clearState: true}` plus a comment ('dispatcher signs in via the demo card... exercises calling') that was never turned into Maestro steps — byte-identical across both platforms and all three captured runs (baseline + flake_rerun_1 + flake_rerun_2). Because clearState wipes the app to the login screen and the flow ends in ~2s ([Passed] in calling.yaml.out.log; junit status=SUCCESS) without signing in or navigating, _capture_mobile_screenshot grabs the login screen and the screenshot judge reports 'Screen shows a login form instead of any call UI'. The fingerprint visual/screen-shows-a-login-form-instead-of-any-call-ui superficially resembles an app calling defect (A-series/X1) but the app was never exercised: the Flutter app has full calling wiring (cometchat_service.dart CometChatUIKit.init+CometChatCalls.init+loginWithAuthToken; call_screen.dart joinSession; call_manager.dart). Compare smoke/flows/delivery/android/calling.yaml, a complete live-verified flow, to see what the logistics flow was supposed to be. Two harness defects: (1) the logistics smoke flows were left as launch-only stubs; (2) the pre-integrate linter lint_calling_spec.py only checks Gate 3 flow-file EXISTENCE (check 2) and applies its [NO-STUB-COVERAGE] anti-stub guard only to Gate 6 choreographies (check 4), so the stub sailed through. RECOMMENDED HARNESS FIX: (a) author the logistics chat/calling flows with real login+navigation+call steps modeled on the delivery flow; (b) extend the [NO-STUB-COVERAGE] check (or add a flow-content lint / a classify_harness_error rule) so any smoke flow whose only command is launchApp is failed as a harness_bug rather than passed and visually judged. This keeps the failure OUT of the app/skills gap ledger — it measures nothing about the skills under review.

debug_agent.root_cause: The logistics Gate 3 smoke flows (smoke/flows/logistics/{android,ios}/{chat,calling}.yaml) are unimplemented stubs: each is 10 lines containing only `- launchApp: {clearState: true}` plus a comment describing steps that were never authored (contrast the complete, live-verified smoke/flows/delivery/android/calling.yaml, ~150 lines). clearState wipes app data so the app boots to the login screen, the flow ends in ~2s ([Passed] in calling.yaml.out.log) without signing in or navigating, and _capture_mobile_screenshot (lib/smoke.py:1016) captures the login screen. The screenshot judge then correctly reports 'login form instead of any call UI' — a false app-calling-defect attribution for what is actually a harness test-scaffold gap. The harness linter lint_calling_spec.py check 2 only verifies flow-file existence, and its [NO-STUB-COVERAGE] hardening (line ~203) was applied only to Gate 6 choreographies, never to Gate 3 smoke flows, so the stub passed pre-integrate lint. The app is fully calling-wired (cometchat_service.dart:103/121/147 init+login, call_screen.dart joinSession, call_manager.dart) and was never exercised.

blockers.issue: MISATTRIBUTION (harness bug): The gate-3 visual failure 'Screen shows a route list, not a chat conversation' on logistics android-ios is caused by an UNAUTHORED Maestro smoke flow, not by the app. chat.yaml (and calling.yaml) contain only `- launchApp: {clearState: true}` plus a prose comment describing the intended steps ('opens the Chats tab, exercises chat') — no tapOn/assertVisible/input exists (flows byte-identical across smoke, flake_rerun_1, flake_rerun_2). The flow never navigates to chat, so it screenshots whatever screen the app happens to show after launch; the same stub yields login (today's android shots), 'Route Board' (dossier + rerun_1 ios), and a blank frame (rerun_1 android calling) across runs — dispositive evidence the flow, not the app, controls the screen. The app is correctly integrated: home_shell.dart:33-39,76 places a one-tap 'Chats' tab on every role opening ChatsInboxScreen (chats_inbox_screen.dart:33) which renders the real CometChatConversations UI Kit over a properly-initialized CometChatService (cometchat_service.dart:44-60, including the X1 CometChatCalls.init split). Remediation is harness-side: author the flow's navigation (tapOn demo 'Dispatcher' card -> tapOn 'Chats' tab -> tapOn a conversation -> assertVisible message composer/bubbles) before judging; do NOT file this as an app/skills/SDK chat defect. This is the same empty-flow-read-as-app-failure class as the prior 'ghost call' misattribution called out in the debug contract.

debug_agent.root_cause: The android/ios smoke 'chat' (and 'calling') Maestro flows are unauthored stubs: the flow body is only `- launchApp: {clearState: true}` followed by a prose comment ('# dispatcher signs in via the demo card, opens the Chats tab, exercises chat.') with ZERO tap/assert/input steps (chat.yaml lines 9-10, byte-identical across smoke + flake_rerun_1 + flake_rerun_2 per diff). The flow never navigates to chat, so it screenshots an arbitrary post-launch screen and the screenshot judge correctly reports 'Route Board / not a chat'. Proof it is the flow and not the app: the same stub yields different screens across runs (login in today's android shots, 'Route Board' in the dossier + rerun_1 ios, blank in rerun_1 android calling) — impossible if the flow controlled navigation. The app is fully chat-wired: a one-tap 'Chats' NavTab on every role (lib/screens/home_shell.dart:33-39,76) opens ChatsInboxScreen which renders the real CometChatConversations UI Kit (lib/screens/chat/chats_inbox_screen.dart:33), backed by a correctly-initialized CometChatService (lib/services/cometchat_service.dart:44-60). The dispatcher's first tab is 'Route Board' (home_shell.dart:86-89), so an auto-restored session lands there; Chats is a separate tab the flow never taps.

verification.reran: artifacts from today's fresh run (pngs regenerated 17:40-17:43) already reproduce deterministically; stub flow confirmed identical across smoke + 2 reruns via diff

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: In cometchat-flutter-v5-conversations/core, add an explicit recipe: before opening a 1:1 thread or placing a direct call, resolve the real User via CometChat.getUser(uid) (or pass the known display name) rather than constructing a stub `User(uid:, name:<UI label>)`. Include a note that the object's `name` is what the thread header and call title render.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: 1:1 direct thread/call shows the UI label as the counterparty's display name (blocker 1).

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Add a cometchat-flutter-v5-calls section for the v5 raw Calls SDK on Flutter (since the prebuilt calls UI Kit is 4.x-bound): document the joinSession + SessionStatusListeners lifecycle and the mandatory callee-side teardown (dismiss call screen on onCallEndedMessageReceived, leaveSession, CometChat.clearActiveCall) needed so a second call can connect — the exact ground the iOS ghost-call / only-first-call-connects known issues occupy.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: docs/skills coverage gap for the v5 raw Calls-SDK call surface and teardown on Flutter.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: In cometchat-flutter-v5-messages/conversations, document the failure/empty-state pattern for entering a group/user thread whose membership may not yet be provisioned (guard + user-visible 'conversation unavailable' fallback), mirroring the inbox EmptyState, so hand-constructed group/user targets degrade gracefully.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Client-fabricated group/user targets pushed into UI Kit widgets with no failure path (blocker 2).

auto_remediations.prompt_snippet:
# Phase B — Targeted Remediation (heal pass)

You already integrated CometChat into this app on branch
`feature/cometchat-integration`. An automated check caught a **specific, known
integration defect** in the code you committed. Your ONLY job now is to fix that
one defect — surgically — and nothing else.

This is **not** a re-integration. Do not redo the integration, do not restructure,
do not "improve" anything that is already working. Treat everything outside the
named defect as frozen.

## What was caught

The following detector(s) fired against your committed code:

- [I1] iOS screen infi

debug_agent.truncation_note: 1 clusters undiagnosed (spawn cap 4) — findings recorded, diagnosis deferred: visual/screen-shows-a-login-role-selection-form-instead-of-any-call

_meta.agent_final_summary:
## INTEGRATION SUMMARY

- **Fixed:** None. The named [I1] defect does not exist in this codebase. This app is **Flutter** (not native SwiftUI), so there is no `@StateObject`/`.task` construct. The two things I1 targets are already correct: singleton ownership is via `ChangeNotifierProvider.value` in `mobile/lib/main.dart:23-26` (no screen owns/re-creates the service), and the side-effecting init is idempotent/guarded in `mobile/lib/services/cometchat_service.dart:45,55` (`connect()` guarded by `_connected || _connecting` and `_inited`), driven from `Session` lifecycle — never from `build()`. I did not make an edit because any change would be fabricated and would violate the "smallest change / fix only the named issue / don't touch working code" rules.
- **Skills/docs used:** None invoked — the defect is a stack mismatch, not an API question, so `docs-mcp`/skills had nothing to correct. **Skill-gap / detector signal:** the I1 catalog entry is authored for the native-iOS (SwiftUI) family and was fired against a **Flutter** iOS build, where the `@StateObject`/`.task` construct cannot exist. The catalog's I1 remediation has no Flutter mapping, and the Flutter code already embodies the "fixed" pattern the entry prescribes.
- **API/class/prop I was unsure existed:** None — I asserted no new APIs.
- **Web-search or guess fallback:** None.
- **Still open:** The `chat.yaml` iOS Maestro smoke failure is **not** attributable to an I1-style re-init loop in this code (verified by tracing every `connect()`/`attach()` caller, every Provider registration, and every chat/call screen's lifecycle). The most likely cause of the smoke failing in ~1s is environmental — CometChat not provisioned in the headless test env, so `connect()` returns `false` and `ChatsInboxScreen` renders the graceful `EmptyState` ("Chat is unavailable") instead of `CometChatConversations`, which the flow can't find. That is a credentials/`.env` provisioning concern (rule 5 keeps creds in gitignored `.env`), not the named code defect, and cannot be confirmed headlessly here.
